$schema: Once Upon A Time — site features built without PDF design source
summary: 35+ features, components, and pages live in production but had no corresponding artifact in the original design PDF set. This inventory exists so the design team can either (a) bless the implementation as-is, (b) draft formal specs to retrofit, or (c) make explicit decisions about the Shopify migration scope.

importance_scale.5: Core to site functioning — removing it breaks a critical journey
importance_scale.4: Important and pervasive — used across many pages

items.title: Site-wide Search
annotations: Search input — reads ?q= from URL and highlights matched terms in results
Result heading + result-type chip — links straight to the source page
Inline match highlight — exact-token matches wrapped in <mark>
items.why: 32-entry hand-built index with token-based scoring, exact-phrase boost, all-tokens-must-match filter, matched-term highlighting, browse-by-section quick links when no query. Header magnifying-glass icon-link on every page is the entry point.
items.design_notes: Needs a dedicated SERP layout spec, suggested-query chips, recent-search history pattern, and empty-state illustration.

items.title: Sign-in / Sign-up Modal
items.why: Self-injecting overlay with role toggle (student / donor), social OAuth buttons (Google, Apple, Email), password visibility toggle, remember-me, forgot-password link, focus trap, ESC-close. Triggered by every Sign-in button via data-open-signin.
items.design_notes: PDFs cover login/signup as full pages; the modal pattern was added so users can authenticate without losing context. Needs spec for error states, OAuth fail recovery, and loading.

items.title: Service Worker + Offline Page
items.why: Precaches the app shell on first load, stale-while-revalidate for same-origin GETs, navigation requests fall back to offline.html when the user is disconnected. Bumped on every deploy that ships changed assets.
items.design_notes: Offline page copy and illustration could use a branded pass — currently uses plain text and a single icon.

items.title: Landing Page Hero Carousel
items.why: Auto-rotating 4-slide hero with keyboard navigation, dot indicators that meet WCAG target-size minimum (24×24 hit area / 8px visual), and rich slide metadata (eyebrow, title, subtitle, lede, CTA with icon, photo).
items.design_notes: PDFs show a single static landing hero. Carousel was added so multiple programs can share the prime slot. Need formal specs for slide-change transitions, autoplay timing, and reduced-motion fallback.

items.title: Programs Page Chip Filter + Search
annotations: Chip filter row — All / Dance / Theater / Acting / Gardening, single-select with aria-pressed
In-page keyword search — filters live, deep-links via ?q=
Program card with track chip — chip text matches the active filter
items.why: Chip row (All / Dance / Theater / Acting / Gardening) and live keyword search above the program cards. Filters both Open for Enrollment and Coming Soon sections; hides empty sections and shows a Clear-Filters affordance when nothing matches. Reads ?q= from URL.
items.design_notes: The PDF programs page has no filtering UI. Pattern is identical to standard e-commerce category pages — would benefit from a Shopify Search & Discovery integration.

items.title: Interactive Class Calendar
items.why: Full month-by-month navigation with real schedule data keyed by month-year. Day cells are colour-coded by class type (rose / garden / blush) and link to enrollment. Lives on the home page next to today's classes.
items.design_notes: PDFs only show a static today's-classes list. Need spec for week view, multi-class days, and time-zone behaviour.

items.title: Toast Notification System
items.why: Stack-managed toasts with four variants (info / success / warning / mock). Used by every data-mock interaction so there is consistent confirmation feedback across the entire site. Auto-dismiss after 3.5s, accessible via aria-live region.
items.design_notes: Variant palette and icons need explicit design tokens. Current implementation is minimal: solid pill, matching brand color, dismissible.

items.title: Generic Confirmation Dialog
items.why: Any element with data-confirm opens a focus-trapped modal with title, body, and labeled CTA button. Used for destructive or stateful actions (cancel program, remove profile, etc.) so users always see a confirm step.
items.design_notes: Needs a destructive variant (red CTA) explicitly specced.

items.title: Settings Dropdown Menu
items.why: Wraps the gear icon in the header with a dropdown: My Profile, Preferences, Class history, Inbox, Search, Accessibility, Sign out. Provides account navigation without forcing a full-page detour to a profile hub.
items.design_notes: Visual treatment is functional but uniform — would benefit from icons per item and section dividers.

items.title: Bookmark Toggle
items.why: data-bookmark on any element toggles localStorage state, swaps the icon between hollow and filled, and emits a toast. Used on program cards, articles, instructor profiles. Persistent across sessions.
items.design_notes: Icon set should be formalised (currently Phosphor bookmark-simple / -fill). Empty / saved-articles dashboards use the same key.

items.title: Share Button
items.why: data-share uses the Web Share API on mobile and falls back to copy-to-clipboard on desktop. Reads optional data-share-url and data-share-title. Used on every program card.
items.design_notes: Copy-success toast copy is a placeholder.

items.title: Cookie Consent Banner
items.why: Auto-injected on first visit, persists acknowledgement to localStorage (ouat:cookies-acked). Decline / Accept all actions with a Learn-more link.
items.design_notes: Copy is generic; legal team should provide final wording. Categorised consent (analytics vs essential) is implied but not specced.

items.title: Auto-Injected Breadcrumbs
items.why: 15-route map auto-injects breadcrumb HTML at the top of detail and transactional pages: newsletter article, instructor profile, enrollment, receipt, gift donation, scholarship, sponsorship, donor wall, impact report, gallery, events, programs, etc.
items.design_notes: Visual treatment is plain; could have a more branded chevron and an expand-on-hover affordance for long paths.

items.title: Skip-to-Main Link
items.why: First focusable element on every page jumps to #main. WCAG 2.1 bypass-blocks compliance.
items.design_notes: Visible-on-focus only; uses brand rose. Designers may want a more visible treatment for low-vision users.

items.title: FAQ Accordion
items.why: data-faq buttons toggle aria-expanded on adjacent panels. Used across the contact / FAQ section and donation questions.
items.design_notes: Open and closed states currently use a simple chevron. PDF showed pink and olive Q&A bubble illustrations that are now in place; expand transition needs spec.

items.title: Gallery Lightbox
items.why: Click any data-lightbox tile to open a full-bleed overlay with the underlying CSS background-image. ESC-to-close, click outside to close.
items.design_notes: Needs prev/next navigation, swipe support, and image caption treatment.

items.title: Skeleton Loaders
items.why: List pages with data-skeleton-list show shimmering placeholder cards for ~600ms before the real content reveals — masking layout shift on slow connections.
items.design_notes: Shimmer animation timing and colour are functional defaults. Need explicit token for skeleton bg / highlight.

items.title: Error / Maintenance / Offline Pages
items.why: Branded fallback pages for server errors, scheduled downtime, and offline navigations. Each carries the canonical header + footer so users can recover or contact support.
items.design_notes: Copy and illustration are placeholders. Tone (apologetic vs reassuring) needs a brand voice pass.

items.title: Dismissable Promo Bar
items.why: Top-of-page strip announcing happening-now and next-class. Dismissal persists to localStorage (promo-dismissed-2026 key) so it does not nag returning visitors.
items.design_notes: Currently a single static message. Needs CMS-driven content for production.

items.title: Dark Mode Toggle
items.why: Setting in the gear menu writes ouat:theme to localStorage; .theme-dark class restyles the surface palette. Currently only the footer and a few surfaces are mapped.
items.design_notes: Dark palette is inferred from the rose/garden tokens — no formal dark spec exists. Would benefit from a full token table.

items.title: Inline Form Validation
items.why: Newsletter and contact forms check empty / email-shaped values before submit. OUAT_setFieldError adds .is-error to the field and emits a sibling .field-error message. Submission goes through Formspree once IDs are wired.
items.design_notes: Error state pattern is consistent but minimal. Need specs for inline help, success state, and submit-pending spinner.

items.title: Schema.org JSON-LD
items.why: Organization, ContactPage, and Course types embedded as JSON-LD so Google can render rich results (sitelinks, FAQ rich answers, course cards in SERPs).
items.design_notes: Invisible to designers but worth flagging — copy choices in the JSON affect how the site appears in search results.

items.title: sitemap.xml + robots
items.why: 26 priority URLs with change-frequency hints for crawlers.
items.design_notes: Generated by hand; should be automated post-Shopify (theme apps emit it).

items.title: Canonical Footer + Sync Tool
annotations: About column — About + Programs links, h3 heading per WCAG hierarchy
Events column — Events + Support Us links matching the PDF spec
Address + socials + legal block — Maps deep-link, real social URLs, Privacy · Terms · Accessibility
items.why: 8 distinct footer variants existed across 47 pages; collapsed to one canonical block (Brand, About / Programs, Events / Support Us, Address + socials + legal). tools/sync-footer.py keeps it in lock-step until the Shopify migration replaces it with a Liquid section.
items.design_notes: Matches the PDF programs-page footer exactly. After Shopify cut-over the script retires.

items.title: data-mock Interaction Layer
items.why: 25 distinct data-mock="feature" values registered and wired. Each click emits a toast describing the action that *will* happen post-Shopify integration. Lets the site demo end-to-end without real backends.
items.design_notes: When backends land, replace toast-only mocks one-by-one. The complete list is documented in mock-system.js.

items.title: Coming Soon Stubs
items.why: data-coming-soon attribute on any element shows a polite placeholder so partial pages still feel intentional.
items.design_notes: Generic copy; replace per-page once the real content lands.

items.title: Header Search Icon-Link
items.why: Magnifying-glass icon-button between Contact and Cart on every page header except programs.html / newsletter.html / search.html (where in-page search already exists). Single canonical entry point to /search.html.
items.design_notes: Currently icon-only on desktop; hidden on mobile by existing media-query rules. Mobile users reach search via the hamburger menu — that link should be added.

items.title: Transactional Confirmation Pages
items.why: End-of-journey screens for enrollment, donation, and waiver flows. No PDFs cover them; they were filled in to complete the user journey end-to-end.
items.design_notes: Need a unified visual treatment with the success / failure pattern. Tone should align with brand voice once the design team blesses one.

items.title: Account Dashboard Pages
items.why: Logged-in surface for managing profile, notification preferences, saved content, message threads, and class history. Some have partial PDF coverage; most do not.
items.design_notes: These pages share visual language with the rest of the site but were filled in beyond the PDF set. Warrant a dedicated account-area design pass.

items.title: Support / Donation Variants
items.why: Donation flow has multiple entry points beyond the single donate.html in the PDF set: scholarships, sponsorships, gift-in-someone's-name, donor recognition wall, and the annual impact report.
items.design_notes: Each is a separate funnel. Worth a strategic decision: are these distinct pages or a single multi-step flow?

items.title: People & Recruitment Pages
items.why: Roster and recruiting surface — instructor list and individual bios, board of directors, open jobs, press kit, and partners directory.
items.design_notes: Visual templates exist but the content model (bio fields, social links, role tags) needs a design system pass before Shopify metaobjects.
